research_question: After treating round6 primarily as a standardization-and-dedup pass rather than genuine alpha expansion, which more orthogonal atomic price-volume mechanisms can still expand the retained keeper pool under the frozen v18 operational contract without colliding with the current canonical alias map or simply remixing existing confirmation and amount-shock clusters?

discovery_contract.builder_rule: each candidate builder may rank exactly one raw signal field and may not combine multiple raw signals inside the same candidate
discovery_contract.source_inspiration_rule: candidate definitions may draw inspiration from Alpha158-style atomic expressions and common price-volume paper mechanisms, but every field must be implemented independently inside this project and must not rely on the neighboring qlib adapter stack

signal_pool.interpretation: stronger positive short-horizon return autocorrelation may encode a serial-dependence mechanism distinct from simple medium-term momentum ranking

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This tests serial dependence directly rather than cumulative trend strength, so it should be treated as a distinct micro-trend mechanism.

signal_pool.interpretation: more persistent amount dynamics may reveal a trading-activity persistence mechanism distinct from one-off amount shocks

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This focuses on persistence in trading activity rather than short-vs-medium amount level displacement.

signal_pool.interpretation: lower concentration of traded amount on down days may encode a downside-pressure mechanism distinct from broad price-volume confirmation

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This isolates downside-flow asymmetry specifically instead of net signed flow.

signal_pool.interpretation: a larger share of recent trading range occurring on up days may capture directional range participation distinct from close-location or breakout proximity

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This measures directional range participation across days rather than intraday close placement within each bar.

signal_pool.interpretation: stronger average intraday drift may encode a session-level trend-quality mechanism distinct from close-to-close momentum

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This isolates open-to-close drift instead of multi-week cumulative trend path.

signal_pool.interpretation: more positively skewed recent return distributions may reveal an asymmetry mechanism distinct from volatility level or downside-tail pressure

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This tests distribution asymmetry rather than just downside-tail magnitude.

signal_pool.interpretation: lower recent amount volatility may encode steadier participation quality distinct from amount level or amount persistence

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This uses amount-path variability instead of turnover-path variability and may capture a different participation-quality mechanism.

signal_pool.interpretation: fewer recent downside gaps may encode an overnight fragility mechanism distinct from post-gap recovery strength

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This focuses on how often downside gaps occur, not how well they recover once they happen.

signal_pool.interpretation: a stronger balance of high-break events versus low-break events may capture structural dominance more directly than distance-to-breakout or distance-to-breakdown signals

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This tests event-frequency balance rather than static distance to a recent boundary.

signal_pool.interpretation: stronger absorption on weak days may encode a downside-resilience mechanism distinct from generic downside recovery or intraday skew signals

dedup_note.independent_budget_reason: This isolates absorption quality on weak sessions rather than average recovery strength across all relevant sessions.

discovery_contract.aggregation_rule: none; this round explicitly forbids combining signals
discovery_contract.missing_data_rule: score nulls are allowed and must be preserved for audit; no imputation or cross-signal fallback is allowed

constants_frozen.selection_contract: same calibrated 0.70 ranking liquidity guard, same TopK=10 width, and same v18 top-15 refresh hysteresis
constants_frozen.portfolio_contract: same v18 refreshed-portfolio implementation until any later family round explicitly changes it

promotion_rule.phase_gate: Only atomic signals that show directionally coherent positive edge in signal-edge diagnostics may advance into a later composability-screening round.
promotion_rule.non_promotion_rule: Alias-like variants, inverted duplicates, or signals whose top slice remains weaker than rank11_20 should be rejected rather than rescued by later overlay or family tweaks.

disallowed_changes: Do not mix multiple raw signals into one candidate inside this round.
Do not change the calibrated 0.70 ranking liquidity guard threshold or guard stage.
Do not change the frozen v18 refresh hysteresis rule.
Do not change benchmark or cost contracts.
Do not add extraction, weight-penalty, turnover-suppression, stability-cap, overlay, or family-composition changes inside this round.
Do not import or depend on the neighboring qlib data adapter layer; this project must implement all candidate fields independently on its own data contracts.
Do not open budget for candidates that are already covered by the canonical alias map unless a new override_reason is explicitly approved.
notes: This round starts after round6 was explicitly reclassified as a standardization-and-dedup step rather than genuine alpha expansion.
The objective is to probe more orthogonal atomic mechanisms instead of continuing to relabel or repackage existing price-volume confirmation and amount-shock clusters.
